notes: THE HANGED MAN 12-card gravity deck. The apartment at 9:43 PM working on Natalie: the small held objects, the static, the chair that hasn't moved, the silence between rings. Effects bias toward SUSPENSION gain and toward making the chair / boot / TV more present until they get addressed.

cards.title: THE CHAIR IS STILL TIPPED
cards.flavor: From the angle of the armchair you can see one of its legs jutting up. You haven't looked at it for an hour. You haven't stopped seeing it.

then.text: You right the chair. The room exhales by a measure.

cards.title: THE BOOT TURNS ON ITS LACES
cards.flavor: A slow rotation. There's no draft. The boot turns anyway.

effects.text: The boot, on its peg. The laces are old enough to creak.

cards.title: THE TV STOPS BEING RANDOM
cards.flavor: Static, then a pattern in the static, then the pattern is your face. Then it's static again.

cards.title: THE KETTLE CLICKS OFF ALONE
cards.flavor: You didn't put the kettle on. The kitchenette light flickers once on the click.

then.text: The kettle is hot. You don't remember pressing the switch.

cards.title: SIMON'S VOICEMAIL PLAYS ITSELF
cards.flavor: Through the TV speaker, which is on no input. The greeting Simon recorded the second week of April.

cards.title: THE PHONE DOESN'T RING
cards.flavor: Not yet. The not-ringing has a texture. You've started knowing that texture.

effects.text: You wait for the phone. The phone keeps not ringing.

cards.title: THE FRIDGE HUMS INTO A WORD
cards.flavor: Not a word; the shape of one. Two syllables. You don't transcribe it. You think you might be able to.

cards.title: THE ARMCHAIR HOLDS YOU A LITTLE TIGHTER
cards.flavor: Same chair. Same upholstery. The way it holds you has been getting more specific each visit.

cards.title: YOU COULD CALL SIMON'S MOTHER
cards.flavor: You have her number. You should have called her on Sunday. It is now Wednesday.

effects.text: You think the number. You don't dial. The phone is in the kitchenette and you are not.

cards.title: THE WINDOW IS JUST A WINDOW
cards.flavor: Tonight the fire-escape blue is exactly the colour it ever has been. The window is not, this evening, doing anything.

effects.text: The window is a window. You note it.

cards.title: THE PHONE RINGS
cards.flavor: Once. Twice. The third ring is the one you decide on.

effects.text: The phone is ringing. ANSWER CALL is in your hand. The phone is in the kitchenette.

cards.title: YOU HAVE NOT MOVED IN AN HOUR
cards.flavor: You notice your hand is cold against your knee in a way that means it has been there since the cooling started.

effects.text: You stand. Blood comes back into your feet. The room is the same room and you are a person in it again.
